Legend — How to read this page
Customer Tiers (based on % of total YTD revenue)
A
Strategic — top 60% of revenue
Highest-value accounts. Any loss or decline here is critical. Prioritise retention.
B
Growth — next 25% of revenue
Mid-tier accounts with growth potential. Target for upsell and deeper relationships.
C
Long-tail — remaining 15%
Smaller accounts. Worth monitoring collectively — can graduate to B with attention.
Status Icons
🟢
Growing
YoY revenue up more than 10% vs same period last year
🟡
Stable
YoY change within ±10% — broadly flat performance
🔴
Declining
YoY revenue down more than 10% — monitor closely or escalate
🆕
New in 2026
No revenue in 2025 same period — brand new customer this year

🌐 Share the Viewer Version Online (Free)
Option A — Netlify Drop (easiest, 30 seconds)
1. Save this .html file to your computer
2. Go to netlify.com/drop
3. Drag the file onto the page → instant permanent URL
4. Share that URL with your team
5. When you have new weekly data: re-process, re-drag the updated file

⚡ Tip: Switch to Viewer mode before sharing — the URL opens in whatever mode was last active.
Option B — GitHub Pages (auto-updates)
1. Create a free GitHub account → New repository → upload this .html as index.html
2. Go to Settings → Pages → Source: main branch
3. GitHub gives you a permanent URL like yourname.github.io/repo
4. Each week: replace the file and commit — viewers see updated data instantly
Admin vs Viewer Mode
🔒 Admin Mode
✓ Upload & overwrite data
✓ Edit all mapping tables
✓ Edit all labels & text
✓ Enter forecast estimates
✓ Export raw data as CSV
✓ View all pages
👁 Viewer Mode
✓ All charts & analytics
✓ Week navigation
✓ All drill-downs
✓ See saved forecasts
✗ No upload/mapping/labels
✗ No forecast editing
💡 How to update weekly: Each week, upload the new Excel file on the Upload Data page (top of Admin nav). The dashboard re-computes everything automatically. Then save the .html and re-deploy to share with viewers.
📤 Upload weekly data — you can overwrite the 2026 dataset or append new weeks. The dashboard will recompute all analytics, charts and tables after upload.

Replace All: Uploads a full fresh dataset (e.g. W01–W15). All previous 2026 data is replaced.
